h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to practice placing the cards and identifying the roles of the numbers on the carbon tile.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [sets the overlay aside and places the cards on the tile] "Top 6: atomic number, identifies carbon. C for the symbol; carbon for the name. Bottom 12.01: atomic mass entry."
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully performs the card placement task independently after the overlay scaffolding is removed.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: [slides the 12.01 card up beside C, leaving the bottom slot empty] "Now one slot has no answer. The symbol space holds C, but the overlay still asks two questions: which number names the element, and which number gives the mass entry? Put one number back, then name the job of 6."
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's question about keeping numbers separate by physically moving a card to create an empty slot, forcing the learner to see the functional necessity of the separation.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: [slides the 12.01 card up beside C, leaving the bottom slot empty] "Now one slot has no answer. The symbol space holds C, but the overlay still asks two questions: which number names the element, and which number gives the mass entry? Put one number back, then name the job of 6."
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The physical manipulation of the cards is highly fitted to the learner's conceptual confusion, providing an immediate visual and interactive proof of why the slots must remain separate.
